title: Fix for application for payment
body:
Non-consolidated implementation is incorrect and needs to be updated  to use RevenueAmount instead of BdgtAppliedAmount

Not sure if hotfix will be required
author: Anthony-Angulo-Code

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Updates the stored procedure used to fetch Prime Contract Change Orders for Applications for Payment (AFP) so the non-consolidated path uses `PotentialCO.RevenueAmount` (instead of `BdgtAppliedAmount`) for scheduled value and unit price.

**Changes:**
- Update `dbo.s_enc_Find_PCCOs_For_AFPs` to return `RevenueAmount` for `ScheduledValue` / `UnitPrice`.
reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

review_comments.body: `base_procs.sql` was modified, but the corresponding generated `Core/SQL/delta_procs.sql` update is not included. Per `docs/ai/BACKEND_CONVENTIONS.md:92`, `delta_procs.sql` is the generated diff used for DB upgrades; without regenerating/committing it, existing environments may not receive this stored proc change.
review_comments.author: Copilot

files.patch:
@@ -55391,8 +55391,8 @@ AS
          pcco.PCCONumber ,
          pcco.ChangeOrderID ,
          p.PCONumber ,
-         p.BdgtAppliedAmount AS ScheduledValue ,
-         p.BdgtAppliedAmount AS UnitPrice ,
+         p.RevenueAmount AS ScheduledValue ,
+         p.RevenueAmount AS UnitPrice ,
          1 AS ScheduledQty ,
          p.PCOID
            /* No need to use list_PrimeContractCO here since we aren't applying security or extracting other information */

diff:
diff --git a/Core/SQL/base_procs.sql b/Core/SQL/base_procs.sql
index 7d626a1490..96ed5e3057 100644
--- a/Core/SQL/base_procs.sql
+++ b/Core/SQL/base_procs.sql
@@ -55391,8 +55391,8 @@ AS
          pcco.PCCONumber ,
          pcco.ChangeOrderID ,
          p.PCONumber ,
-         p.BdgtAppliedAmount AS ScheduledValue ,
-         p.BdgtAppliedAmount AS UnitPrice ,
+         p.RevenueAmount AS ScheduledValue ,
+         p.RevenueAmount AS UnitPrice ,
          1 AS ScheduledQty ,
          p.PCOID
            /* No need to use list_PrimeContractCO here since we aren't applying security or extracting other information */
